mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job gains above 130,000 indicate robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 130,000, or the 20d return distribution shows no significant positive skew.

mechanism_narrative: Above-trend housing starts (HOUST > 2000) signal strong demand for residential construction, which correlates with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in 50%+ of triggered periods, or HOUST's predictive power for SPX returns falls below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO > 115) and rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 190) signals strong supply-side momentum and inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The combined INDPRO-CPIAUCSL signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in 50%+ of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising FEDFUNDS above 3% and elevated CPIAUCSL > 195 signal higher discount rates and inflation fears, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction fails to predict negative SPX returns in 50%+ of triggered periods, or the signal's IC drops below -0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent demand-side strength, as measured by GDP growth (GDPC1 > 10900), drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sustained corporate earnings growth.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 60d returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 exceeds 10900, or the 60d return distribution exhibits no significant positive skew.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) and strong job gains (PAYEMS > 130000) signals a tight labor market that drives corporate pricing power, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction fails to predict positive SPX returns in 50%+ of triggered periods, or the signal's IC drops below 0.15 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production (INDPRO > 115) indicates robust supply-side capacity utilization, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate margins.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 5d returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 115, or the 5d return distribution exhibits no significant positive skew.
